title: feat(code-review): rebuild PR detail header to match mock
rationale: has confirmed FP from bot ('BigQuery-powered metrics' + formatNumber identical claim) — recurrence target
body:
## Summary

Aligns the per-PR detail page header (`/code-review/pulls/:prId`) with the design spec at `scratch/code-review-detail-pages-mocks.html` lines 1205–1224. Item (1) of a 6-item punch list — header card + 4-stat grid + action buttons. Subsequent items (timeline, reviews accordion, nested findings tables, per-finding action menu, diff stats wiring beyond raw display, etc.) tracked separately.

## What changed

- Header is now a card (border, radius, padding) instead of a plain text block
- Title row: PR # + title + state badge + `↗ View on GitHub` (links to `pr.url`) + `↻ Run another review` (placeholder, disabled — wiring is item 4)
- Sub-row: avatar + author · `head → base` (mono) · diff stats (`+X / −Y · N files`) when the webhook captured `additions`/`deletions`/`changedFiles`
- Replaced the single `2 Reviews` stat tile with a 4-column meta grid:
  - **Opened** — relative time of `pr.openedAt`
  - **First review** — latency from PR open to earliest review's `startedAt` (or `—`)
  - **Review runs** — count + relative time of latest review (or `—`)
  - **Findings** — `N total · M actionable · K FP` derived from existing finding statuses (`actionable` = status ∈ {pending, applied, not-applied}, `FP` = status === 'irrelevant')
- Merged/closed timestamp moved to a small line below the grid

## Out of scope (tracked for follow-ups)

2. Reviews accordion cards (currently a flat list)
3. Findings nested under each review (currently a global flat list)
4. Per-finding `···` action menu (mark TP / mark FP / dismiss)
5. Timeline strip (PR opened → reviews → merged)
6. Wiring "Run another review" to a real endpoint

## Test plan

- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.app.json` — clean
- [x] `npm run lint` — 0 errors (only pre-existing fast-refresh warnings)
- [x] `vitest run src/pages/code-review/__tests__/` — 28/28 pass (8 detail page, 8 list page, 12 modal), all with a11y assertions
- [ ] Visual eyeball against the mock at `scratch/code-review-detail-pages-mocks.html#pr-detail`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

botReviews.body:
## Code Review — PR #1788

**2 files changed** · +237 −45 · 3 subagents (Security, Consistency, Bug-Hunter)

| # | Sev | File | Line | Rule | Summary |
|---|-----|------|------|------|---------|
| 1 | **MAJOR** | `CodeReviewPullDetailPage.tsx` | 69 | bug/negative-duration | `formatDuration` produces garbled output for negative `ms` values. When clock skew causes `firstReviewLatencyMs` to be negative, the function returns strings like `"-1m"` or `"-1h 30m"`, which will confuse users in the "First review" tile. Add a guard: `if (ms <= 0) return '< 1m';` or `ms = Math.max(ms, 0);` |
| 2 | **MAJOR** | `CodeReviewPullDetailPage.tsx` | 69 | consistency/duplicate-utility | `formatDuration` is copy-pasted from 10+ other files (SkillUsageDashboard, KnowledgeActivity, InsightsExecutions, WorkflowRunDetailPage, WorkflowRunsListPage, TaskExecutionHistoryPage, TaskExecutionViewerPage, RepoSkillsPage, etc.). Extract to `ui/src/utils/formatDuration.ts` and import — matching the pattern used for `formatRelativeTime`. |
| 3 | **MINOR** | `CodeReviewPullDetailPage.tsx` | 65 | consistency/duplicate-utility | `formatNumber` is a local copy of an identical function in `UsageDashboard.tsx`. Extract to `ui/src/utils/formatNumber.ts` and import from both sites. |
| 4 | **MINOR** | `CodeReviewPullDetailPage.tsx` | 112 | bug/undefined-status | `actionableCount = findings.length - fpCount` treats findings with `status === undefined` as actionable. Define actionable explicitly: `findings.filter(f => ['pending','applied','not-applied'].includes(f.status)).length`. |
| 5 | **MINOR** | `CodeReviewPullDetailPage.tsx` | 164 | bug/unwanted-space | The `{' '}` between additions and deletions spans renders unconditionally. When `pr.additions` is null but `pr.deletions` is present, you get a stray leading space (`" −5"` instead of `"−5"`). Move the separator inside a conditional. |
| 6 | **MINOR** | `CodeReviewPullDetailPage.tsx` | 69 | bug/zero-duration | `formatDuration(0)` returns `"0m"`. A user-facing display of `"< 1m"` would be clearer. |
| 7 | **MINOR** | `CodeReviewPullDetailPage.tsx` | 536 | consistency/dead-code | `onRunAnotherReview` prop is never wired at the call site — the "Run another review" button is a permanently disabled stub with no tracking issue. Either wire it up, add a TODO with an issue number, or remove the button. |
| 8 | **NIT** | `CodeReviewPullDetailPage.tsx` | 94 | perf/double-map-reduce | `earliestReviewStart` and `latestReviewStart` each do `.map().reduce()` over the same array. A single reduce or `Math.min(...times)` / `Math.max(...times)` avoids the intermediate allocation. |

**Security**: No findings. The `pr.url` is rendered as `<a href>` with `rel="noopener noreferrer"` — correct. No XSS vectors, no secrets, no auth changes.

---

Reviewed SHA: d2c04e1cb98b8b20b9007eab7ff3f07f0034d953

## Code Review — PR #1788

**5 findings** (2 MAJOR · 2 MINOR · 1 NIT)

---

### MAJOR — `formatDuration` sub-minute durations show "0m"

**File:** `ui/src/utils/formatDuration.ts:22–24`

Any duration in `(0, 60_000)` ms is positive so it bypasses the `≤ 0` guard, but `Math.floor(ms / 60_000)` yields 0, producing the confusing string `"0m"`. The doc comment acknowledges the intent to show `"< 1m"` for these cases. Will surface whenever first-review latency is under a minute.

```ts
// current
if (ms <= 0) return '< 1m';
const minutes = Math.floor(ms / 60_000);
if (minutes < 60) return `${minutes}m`;

// suggested
if (ms < 60_000) return '< 1m';
```

---

### MAJOR — `new Date(NaN).toISOString()` can throw RangeError

**File:** `ui/src/pages/code-review/CodeReviewPullDetailPage.tsx:83–85`

If any review's `startedAt` is a present-but-invalid date string, `getTime()` returns `NaN`, which propagates through the `reduce` into `latestReviewStart`. Because `NaN !== null` is true, the ternary enters the branch that calls `new Date(NaN).toISOString()`, which throws an unhandled `RangeError`, crashing the component.

```ts
// current
latestReviewStart !== null
  ? `${reviews.length} (latest ${formatRelativeTime(new Date(latestReviewStart).toISOString())})`
  : String(reviews.length);

// suggested: guard with Number.isFinite
Number.isFinite(latestReviewStart)
  ? `${reviews.length} (latest ${formatRelativeTime(new Date(latestReviewStart).toISOString())})`
  : String(reviews.length);
```

---

### MINOR — `formatDuration` produces `"NaNd"` on NaN input

**File:** `ui/src/utils/formatDuration.ts:21–29`

If `formatDuration` receives `NaN` (e.g. from an invalid timestamp subtraction), none of the numeric comparisons match (`NaN < 60` is false), and the function falls through to return `"NaNd"`. Add a `Number.isFinite` guard at the top to prevent display corruption.

```ts
// suggested
if (!Number.isFinite(ms)) return '—';
```

---

### MINOR — Doc comment claims 9 copies but count is inaccurate

**File:** `ui/src/utils/formatDuration.ts:18–19`

The comment states *"9 other files still have local copies — those will be migrated in a follow-up PR"*, but this count was not verified in this PR and may be stale or inaccurate, misleading future maintainers about the scope of the migration.

---

### NIT — Doc example contradicts implementation

**File:** `ui/src/utils/formatDuration.ts:4`

The example `formatDuration(45_000) → "0m" ← clamped via the zero-guard below ⇒ "< 1m"` is self-contradictory — it shows both `"0m"` and `"< 1m"` as the output. Update to reflect whichever behaviour is intended (likely `"< 1m"` once the MAJOR fix above is applied).

---

Reviewed SHA: 2778a4387b346e88f7e75b775515e0b9b9085f8d

findings.description: formatDuration produces '-1m' for negative ms (clock skew on firstReviewLatencyMs)
findings.reason: extracted to ui/src/utils/formatDuration.ts with ms<=0 guard returning '< 1m'
partial — extracted to shared util and consumed here. Other 8 files defer to a follow-up because signatures vary
identical claim is wrong — UsageDashboard's has K/M compact abbreviations, ours is plain toLocaleString. Created shared util for this page; UsageDashboard kept as-is because semantics differ

findings.description: actionableCount treats findings with status===undefined as actionable
findings.reason: current code is correct — the existing dashboard treats undefined as 'pending' (line 366: f.status??'pending'); changing would create mismatch with status-filter pill counts

findings.description: stray leading space in diff stats when additions is null and deletions is present
findings.reason: moved separator inside additions+deletions conjunction conditional

findings.description: onRunAnotherReview is permanently disabled stub with no tracking issue
findings.reason: added inline comment referencing punch-list item 4 in PR description

findings.description: earliestReviewStart and latestReviewStart each do .map().reduce() over the same array
findings.reason: real on tiny array (<=30 reviews per cap); two-pass code is more readable than Math.min/max spread which needs empty-array fallback

findings.description: Sub-minute durations (0 < ms < 60_000) bypass <= 0 guard and return '0m'
findings.reason: replaced <= 0 guard with < 60_000 to cover all three degenerate cases

findings.description: Invalid startedAt → getTime() returns NaN → reduce propagates → new Date(NaN).toISOString() throws RangeError
findings.reason: filter Number.isFinite before Math.min/max — NaN can't reach toISOString()

findings.description: NaN input falls through every comparison and returns 'NaNd'
findings.reason: added Number.isFinite guard at top returning '—'

findings.description: Doc comment claims 9 other files have local copies but count is unverified/drifted
findings.reason: removed specific count from docstring — now says 'other files' without naming a number
rewrote example to → '< 1m' only; added explicit examples for 0, negative, and NaN

findings.description: <h1> replaced with <span> — page loses its only heading, breaking a11y
findings.reason: restored <h1 class=crpd-header-title> wrapping PR # and title; added margin:0 to keep layout identical

findings.description: formatDuration returns 0m for sub-minute positive values
findings.reason: already fixed in round-2 commit b3a8c03f via < 60_000 guard

findings.description: actionableCount uses subtraction instead of explicit status filter
findings.reason: intentional — undefined-status findings are pending (line 366 f.status ?? pending), bot's filter would exclude them and break meta/pill count agreement
already fixed in round-2 commit b3a8c03f via rewritten example block

findings.description: Run another review button always disabled, shipped as dead UI
findings.reason: already addressed in round-1 commit 2778a438 via inline TODO comment referencing punch-list item 4
